seoTitle: Make General Journal Entries in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to post a balanced journal entry in QuickBooks Online for adjustments like depreciation or owner's equity without breaking your ledger.
intro: Some adjustments, like depreciation or owner's equity changes, don't fit standard invoices or expenses and require a manual journal entry. This guide walks through creating a balanced double-entry journal in QuickBooks Online so your books stay accurate.

steps.heading: Open the Journal Entry form
steps.body: Click the + New button and select Journal Entry from the Other column to open a blank double-entry form.

steps.heading: Set the date and entry number
steps.body: Enter the correct transaction date and assign a unique journal number for tracking.

steps.heading: Enter your debit line
steps.body: Choose the target account for the first row and enter the value under the Debits column, adding a memo for context.

steps.heading: Enter your balancing credit line
steps.body: On the second row, select the offsetting account and let QuickBooks calculate the matching value under Credits.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Confirm total debits equal total credits, then click Save and Close to post the entry to your ledger.

faq.question: What happens if my debits and credits don't match?
faq.answer: QuickBooks will flag a validation error and won't let you save the entry until the totals balance.

faq.question: Can I attach a name or customer to a journal line?
faq.answer: Yes, you can add a name in the Name field on each row to tie the entry to a specific customer, vendor, or employee.

faq.question: How do I check that a journal entry posted correctly?
faq.answer: Run a Journal Report or Transaction Detail report to confirm the entry appears with the correct account impacts.

faq.question: Can journal entries be edited later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reopen and edit a journal entry from the register, though it's best practice to keep a clear audit trail of changes.
